![][image1]  
**Organised by GoodHub SEA in partnership with**   
**Open Government Products** 

*Part of the Build for Good Stewards programme* 

**Welcome**

Congratulations on being selected to participate in The Good Hack 2026\! We are thrilled to have you on board.

The Good Hack is a 2.5-day civic tech hackathon where builders, and changemakers come together to create technology that makes a real difference — for nonprofits, the elderly, and communities across Southeast Asia. 

This playbook is your go-to guide. It covers everything you need to know to prepare, what to expect during the event, and what happens after. Please read it carefully and keep it handy.

**Event at a Glance**

| Event | The Good Hack 2026 |
| :---- | :---- |

| Dates | Friday 8 May – Sunday 10 May 2026 |
| :---- | :---- |

| Venue | Open Government Products (OGP) Office 51 Bras Basah Rd, \#04-08 Lazada One, Singapore 189554 |
| :---- | :---- |

| Organisers | GoodHub SEA × Open Government Products |
| :---- | :---- |

| Programme | The Good Hack (Build for Good Stewards) |
| :---- | :---- |

| Team Size | 4 – 5 participants per team |
| :---- | :---- |

| Capacity | 100 participants |
| :---- | :---- |

**Hackathon Themes**

The Good Hack 2026 centres on the theme of Ageing Society. Participants will tackle one of two problem statements:

**Problem Statement 1: Empowering Caregivers**

How might we enable and empower caregivers who are caring for seniors or PWDs so that they can alleviate caregiver fatigue and burnout?

**Problem Statement 2: Streamlining Eldercare** 

How might we reduce manual and time-consuming tasks within the eldercare ecosystem so that more seniors are well-supported?

**Solutions should address real challenges** faced by nonprofits, social service agencies (e.g., Lions Befrienders, Care Corner, RSVP Singapore), and elderly communities, with the potential to scale across similar cities in Southeast Asia.

**Before the Hack**

Here is how to make the most of your preparation time in the weeks leading up to the event.

**Understand the Problem Space**

1. Take time to research the problem statement you are most interested in.   
2. Talk to people affected by the problems.   
3. Understand the landscape of existing solutions. 

**Some Prompts For Your Research**

1. Look into the daily realities of family caregivers and professional carers. What tools and support systems do they lack? How can technology lighten their load?  
2. Explore the operational challenges faced by eldercare nonprofits and social service agencies (e.g., Lions Befrienders, Care Corner)  
3. Speak to elderly family members or neighbours. Read up on Singapore's ageing population policies. Consider how similar challenges play out in other Southeast Asian countries like Indonesia, Malaysia, Vietnam. 

**Get Familiar with the Tools**

You will have access to the following resources during the hackathon. We recommend setting up accounts and getting familiar with these tools beforehand:

| Tool | Details |
| :---- | :---- |
| **OpenAI** | •⁠  ⁠$50 in API Credits (Can be used for Codex & API services) •⁠  ⁠⁠One month of ChatGPT Plus (Can be used for Codex) |
| **Lovable** | 100 credits per participant (one-month validity). Codes must be redeemed within one week of distribution.  |
| **Cloudflare** | Free-tier access to SEA-Lion, a Southeast Asian large language model. |
| **Google** | $25 credit for Google Cloud Platform |

* **You may find the tech tools guide on how to activate your AI credits [here](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1UnJpXfXxQi8bCg5TKWd1hAfbfHcpzlPd8vJKfv095RY/edit?usp=sharing). All credits are personal and cannot be shared.**  
* You are free to use any other technology stack, languages, or frameworks in addition to the tools above. There are no restrictions.

**What to Bring**

* Your laptop and charger 

* A reusable water bottle

* Comfortable clothing 

* An open mind, a collaborative spirit, and a readiness to build\!

**Team Formation on Day 1**

Teams must have 4 to 5 members. You have two options:

1. Come with a pre-formed team. If you already have your team, make sure everyone is accepted individually.

2. Form a team. If you are joining solo or with an incomplete team, we will run structured icebreaking and team formation activities on Day 1

**Submit your team details by 11:59 PM on Friday 8 May.**  

**Programme Schedule**

**Program Outline**

* **Day 1**: Friday 8 May — **Kickoff Evening** (6:30 PM – 10:00 PM)  
* **Day 2:** Saturday 9 May — **Build Day** (8:30 AM – 5:30 PM)  
* **Day 3:** Sunday 10 May — **Demo Day** (8:30 AM – 5:30 PM)

**Important Deadlines to Note**

| To-do Tasks | Deadline / Time to note |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Team formation** | By today (8 May), 11.59pm |
| **Draw manual lots for Demo Day sequence** | Day 2 (9 May), 5.30pm (After Hacking Session \#3) |
| **Submission of solution and MVP** | Form will be shared on Day 2\.  Submit by Day 3 (10 May), 1pm. |
| **Ah Gong Ah Ma Favourite (Audience vote)** | Voting details will be shared on Day 3\. Submit on Day 3 (10 May), 4.30pm (After Demo) |
| **Event feedback survey on Day 3** | Day 3, 5.30pm |

See the full program schedule and speakers lineup at:

* Programme schedule: [https://hack.goodhubsea.org/programme](https://hack.goodhubsea.org/programme)  
* Speakers’ lineup: [https://hack.goodhubsea.org/speakers](https://hack.goodhubsea.org/speakers)

**Solution Requirements**

Your project must meet **ALL** of the following criteria:

1. **Coded solution.** Your project must be built with actual code. No pure no-code submissions. AI-assisted tools like Lovable and vibe-coding workflows are strongly encouraged. 

2. **Can be deployed and demonstrated.** Your solution must be deployed with working infrastructure. You should be able to demonstrate that your APIs are working via your terminal or IDE during the live demo.

3. **Open-sourced.** All projects must be open-sourced on GitHub or equivalent. This is a core requirement. The spirit of The Good Hack is to create solutions that can be maintained and grown by the GoodHub SEA community.

4. **Benefits nonprofits / the elderly.** Your solution should enable nonprofits or directly benefit elderly communities.

5. **Singapore-first, SEA-scalable.** Start with a Singapore-based problem, then consider how the solution could be adapted to cross-cultural contexts and/or deployed in other Southeast Asian cities.

**Demo Day Presentation Format**

**Each team has 10 minutes.** 5 minutes to present, followed by 5 minutes for questions from judges. 

**Be concise in your pitch.** 

Your presentation should include:

* Pitch Deck: A slide deck in PDF format covering the problem, your value proposition and  your solution  
* Live Demo: Show your working prototype. Demonstrate deployed infrastructure and working APIs via your terminal, IDE, or browser.

**Judging Criteria & Awards**

**Evaluation Rubric**

Projects are evaluated by a panel of judges across five criteria. Each criterion is scored on a scale of 1 \- 5\. 

| Criterion | Weight | What Judges Look For |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Problem-Solution Fit** | 35% | How deeply you understand the eldercare challenge and how directly your solution tackles real user pain points |
| **Scales Across Cultures & Borders** | 15% | How inclusively your solution serves Singapore's elderly across languages, dialects, and digital literacy — and whether it could extend to a neighbouring SEA city |
| **Long-term Sustainability** | 15% | Whether there's a credible path to keeping this alive beyond the hackathon — funding, partnerships, or revenue |
| **Go-To Market Strategy** | 15% | Clarity on who your first users are, how you'd reach them, and what gets them to adopt |
| **Innovation & Creativity** | 10% | Originality of the solution and creative approach to solving the challenge |
| **Presentation & Storytelling** | 10% | Clarity of pitch, quality of demo, and how you handle Q\&A |

**Judging Criteria & Scoring System**

# **Problem-Solution Fit (35%)**

*How well the solution addresses the specific challenge or theme.*

| Score | Description |
| :---: | :---- |
| 5 | Exceptional alignment with challenge; deeply understands user pain points; solution directly tackles core issues |
| 4 | Strong problem-solution fit; clear understanding of the challenge; addresses key pain points effectively |
| 3 | Reasonable fit; addresses the problem but may miss some important aspects or user needs |
| 2 | Weak alignment; solution only tangentially related to the challenge or misunderstands core problems |
| 1 | Poor fit; solution doesn’t meaningfully address the stated challenge |

# **Scalability Across Cultures & Borders (15%)**

*How well the solution serves Singapore’s diverse elderly population across languages, dialects, literacy levels, and cultural contexts — and how readily that thinking extends to SEA cities.*

| Score | Description |
| :---: | :---- |
| 5 | **Applicable beyond Singapore;** demonstrates clear thinking on how the solution extends to at least one neighbouring SEA city or context; strong multilingual support within Singapore as a baseline; cultural and accessibility considerations translate meaningfully across borders |
| 4 | Strong accessibility within Singapore across multiple official languages and at least one dialect; thoughtful inclusion of low-literacy and low-digital-literacy users; some early thinking on how it could extend regionally |
| 3 | Reasonable multilingual or dialect support within Singapore; addresses some accessibility needs but limited to the local context; no consideration of regional applicability |
| 2 | Limited accessibility thinking; primarily English-only or assumes digitally-confident users; narrow cultural lens within Singapore |
| 1 | Highly local and narrow; no consideration for dialects, multilingual users, or accessibility; serves only a specific subset of Singapore’s elderly |

**Go-to-Market (15%)**

*Clarity and feasibility of the team’s strategy to launch, acquire users, and reach the target market.*

| Score | Description |
| :---: | :---- |
| 5 | Highly compelling GTM plan; specific channels, partners, and launch sequence; demonstrates deep user and market understanding; clearly identifies beachhead and expansion path |
| 4 | Strong GTM thinking; realistic acquisition strategy; identified target users and credible early channels |
| 3 | Basic GTM plan; some thinking on user acquisition but lacks specificity or feasibility in parts |
| 2 | Weak GTM strategy; vague channels or unrealistic assumptions about user uptake |
| 1 | No meaningful GTM plan; unclear how the solution would reach users |

# **Long-term Project Sustainability (15%)**

*Feasibility of bringing a solution to market and sustaining it over time.*

| Score | Description |
| :---: | :---- |
| 5 | Sustainable business model; strong potential for significant social/economic impact beyond the pilot |
| 4 | Viable business case; demonstrable impact potential |
| 3 | Basic business thinking; impact potential unclear |
| 2 | Weak business case; unrealistic assumptions; limited impact potential |
| 1 | No viable business model; unlikely to create meaningful impact |

# 

# 

# 

# 

# **Innovation & Creativity (10%)**

*Originality of the solution and creative approach to solving the challenge.*

| Score | Description |
| :---: | :---- |
| 5 | Highly innovative solution with novel approach; demonstrates exceptional creative thinking that reimagines the problem space |
| 4 | Strong originality with fresh perspectives; builds meaningfully on existing solutions |
| 3 | Moderate innovation; some creative elements but largely follows conventional approaches |
| 2 | Limited originality; mostly derivative of existing solutions |
| 1 | Lacks innovation; replicates common approaches without differentiation |

# **Presentation & Communication / Storytelling (10%)**

*Quality of demo, clarity of pitch, and team’s ability to communicate their solution.*

| Score | Description |
| :---: | :---- |
| 5 | Compelling presentation; clear articulation of problem, solution, and value; excellent demo; handles questions confidently |
| 4 | Strong presentation; good storytelling; effective demo; responds well to questions |
| 3 | Adequate presentation; gets key points across but lacks polish or clarity in some areas |
| 2 | Weak presentation; unclear messaging; demo issues; struggles with questions |
| 1 | Poor presentation; fails to communicate core value; significant presentation problems |

**Awards**

**Winning Teams**

1. Most Impactful — The solution with the greatest potential to improve lives.

2. Most Innovative Use of Technology — The most creative and original application of available tools and technologies.

3. Best Cultural & Regional Scalability — The most thoughtful and realistic approach to cross-culture and cross-border solutioning

**Special Award**

* "Ahgong/Ahma's Favourite" — Chosen by audience vote during judges' deliberation. All participants (except your own team) can vote for their favourite project.

All participants will receive e-certificates and a potential opportunity to take your opportunity further with grant partners. Winning groups receive additional prizes. Bonus gifts for participants who join our icebreaker games\!

**Logistics & Practical Info**

**Venue**

Open Government Products (OGP) Office

51 Bras Basah Rd, \#04-08 Lazada One, Singapore 189554

* **A QR code for venue entry will be shared 1–2 days before the event** via email

* The venue is wheelchair accessible.

* Parking is available at the venue.

**Food & Refreshments**

Meals and refreshments will be provided at the event:

* Day 1 : Welcome Dinner Reception

* Day 2 : Lunch, and light refreshments during breaks

* Day 3 : Lunch, and light refreshments during breaks

Please ensure your dietary requirements (halal, vegetarian, allergies) were indicated in your registration form. If you missed this, please contact the organising team as soon as possible.

**Communication**

The official communication channel for the hackathon is Telegram. 

Join this channel: [https://t.me/+8k\_Gg4aZHGQ3OWQ1](https://t.me/+8k_Gg4aZHGQ3OWQ1)

All important updates, announcements, and logistical info will go through this channel. Please join promptly once invited.

**Swag**

All participants will receive laptop stickers and a Build For Good laptop stand.

**After the Hack**

The Good Hack does not end when the event is over. Here is what to expect:

**Open-Source Continuation**

All projects from The Good Hack are open-sourced and can continue to be developed as part of GoodHub SEA's community projects. 

If your solution shows real promise, it could be maintained by the volunteer community and potentially introduced to government agencies or adopted by nonprofits in Singapore and across Southeast Asia.

**Next Steps**

* OGP Support: If your solution is a viable pilot, OGP may help introduce it to relevant government agencies for further development and adoption.

* GoodHub SEA Community: Join the GoodHub SEA volunteer pool to continue contributing to tech-for-good projects within Southeast Asia.

* Portfolio & Impact: Your hackathon project is portfolio-worthy work. Showcase it on your LinkedIn and resume as a real-world civic tech project.

* Stay Connected: Keep in touch with your teammates, mentors, and fellow participants. The relationships you build here can lead to future collaborations\! 

**Frequently Asked Questions**

**Q: Do I need to attend all 3 days?**

Yes, full attendance across all 3 days is expected and will be tracked. Day 1 is the evening kickoff and team formation, you must be present to form or finalise your team. Days 2 and 3 are the core hacking, presentation, and awards days.

**Q: What skills does my team need?**

A well-rounded team typically has a mix of technical (coding, deployment) and non-technical skills (problem framing, user research, storytelling, domain expertise). Your solution must be coded and deployed, so having at least one developer is essential.

**Q: Can we use any technology stack?**

Yes. There are no restrictions on programming language, framework, or platform. You are encouraged to leverage the provided AI tools (OpenAI, Lovable, SEA-Lion via Cloudflare) but are not required to use them.

**Q: Can mentors help outside of scheduled hours?**

During the official mentorship check-in window (3:00–5:00 PM on Day 2), mentors will be available. Whether they continue beyond 5:00 PM is entirely at their own discretion. Do not assume availability outside scheduled hours.

**Q: What if I have dietary restrictions I did not mention?**

Please reach out to the organising team as soon as possible at hello@goodhubsea.org so we can accommodate your needs.

**Contact & Support**

Have a question not answered here? Reach out to the GoodHub SEA team:

**Email: [hello@goodhubsea.org](mailto:hello@goodhubsea.org)**

**See you on 8 May. Let’s build something good\!**

GoodHub SEA × Open Government Products  |  Build for Good Stewards |
